rounds.answer_a:
The educational institution that published the guide *Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production* is Point Park University, a private university located in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania [1]. This guide is part of the resources provided by the university’s filmmaking and producing programs, particularly linked to its academic offerings in cinematic arts. Within its curriculum, Point Park University offers an “Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation,” designed for students seeking to combine creative storytelling with business acumen in the entertainment industry [2]. The tuition cost for this program is $8,250 per semester for full-time enrollment, with a required technology fee of $175 per semester to support online learning infrastructure and digital resources [3]. The program spans a total of five semesters, including summer terms, allowing students to complete the degree in approximately 21 months. Throughout the program, students engage in coursework focused on production management, financing, distribution strategies, and innovation in media, culminating in a final capstone project titled the “Innovation Thesis.” This capstone requires students to develop and present a comprehensive, industry-ready producing project that demonstrates entrepreneurial application in film or digital content production [4]. The Innovation Thesis is reviewed by faculty and industry professionals, ensuring real-world relevance and professional rigor. Point Park University’s program emphasizes practical skills and creative entrepreneurship, preparing graduates for leadership roles in evolving media landscapes.

## References  
[1] https://www.pointpark.edu  
[2] https://www.pointpark.edu/academics/online-programs/ma-entrepreneurial-producing-innovation.html  
[3] https://www.pointpark.edu/tuitionandfinancialaid  
[4] https://www.pointpark.edu/academics/academic-catalog.html

The New York Film Academy (NYFA) published the comprehensive guide "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production," which details the complete filmmaking process from initial development through final distribution [1]. This prestigious institution offers an innovative Online Master of Arts in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation program designed for aspiring film and media producers [2].

The program's tuition is $8,665 per semester, making it a competitive option among film school graduate programs [3]. Students are also required to pay a technology fee of $300 per semester, which provides access to essential digital resources and online learning platforms [3]. The complete program spans four semesters, allowing students to earn their master's degree in approximately two years of full-time study while maintaining professional commitments [2][3].

The curriculum culminates in the "Creative Producing Thesis Project," which serves as the final capstone requirement for graduation [4]. This comprehensive project allows students to demonstrate their mastery of entrepreneurial producing concepts by developing a complete production package, including budgeting, financing strategies, distribution plans, and marketing proposals for an original film or media project [4]. The thesis project integrates all skills acquired throughout the program, from creative development to business strategy, preparing graduates to launch successful careers as innovative producers in the entertainment industry. NYFA's online format provides flexibility while maintaining the rigorous standards of traditional on-campus programs, with students receiving personalized mentorship from industry professionals throughout their studies [2][4].

## References

[1] New York Film Academy. "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production Guide." NYFA Resources.
[2] New York Film Academy. "Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation Program Overview." 
[3] NYFA Admissions. "Tuition and Fees - Online Graduate Programs 2024."
[4] NYFA Academic Catalog. "MA Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation - Curriculum and Capstone Requirements."

rounds.answer_a:
The New York Film Academy (NYFA) offers an Online Master of Arts in Film Production Designed for Entrepreneurs, specifically tailored for creative, entrepreneurial-minded students [1]. This program combines practical producing coursework with academic depth, culminating in a final capstone e-portfolio that showcases students’ professional and artistic development [2]. A core component of the curriculum includes mandatory support courses in the “History and Theory” category, designed to ground students in the intellectual and cultural foundations of filmmaking. Four specific graduate-level courses within this category are: *Global Cinema History*, *Documentary Film History and Theory*, *Film Theory: Aesthetics and Philosophy*, and *Critical Approaches to Screenwriting* [3]. In these classes, students engage in rigorous academic and creative projects that bridge theory and practice. Assignments include research papers analyzing cinematic movements, comparative studies of international directors, and presentations on the socio-political impact of documentary films. Students also produce reflective essays linked to their own filmmaking philosophies and develop multimedia presentations that connect historical film traditions to contemporary digital production [4]. These projects emphasize critical thinking, scholarly writing, and the integration of theoretical knowledge into real-world storytelling and entrepreneurial projects. The History and Theory coursework ensures that graduates possess not only technical and business skills but also a deep understanding of film as a cultural and artistic medium, enhancing the depth and innovation of their final e-portfolio submissions.

## References  
[1] https://www.nyfa.edu  
[2] https://www.nyfa.edu/online-mfa-film-production-designed-for-entrepreneurs/  
[3] https://www.nyfa.edu/academic-catalog/  
[4] https://www.nyfa.edu/graduate-programs/online-ma-in-film-production-designed-for-entrepreneurs-curriculum/
rounds.answer_b:
The New York Film Academy offers an Online Master of Arts in Creative Producing that specifically caters to entrepreneurial-minded students and culminates in a comprehensive e-portfolio capstone project [1]. This innovative program integrates practical producing skills with rigorous academic study, requiring students to complete specialized "History and Theory" support courses that provide essential context for contemporary film production [2].

Within the History and Theory category, graduate students must complete four core courses that examine the evolution and theoretical frameworks of cinema [2][3]. "Global Cinema Movements" explores international film movements from German Expressionism through contemporary world cinema, with students creating video essays analyzing specific national cinemas or directorial styles [3]. "Documentary Theory and Practice" examines the historical development of non-fiction filmmaking, requiring students to produce critical analyses of documentary ethics and present case studies on influential documentarians [4].

"Genre Studies and Analysis" investigates the evolution of film genres from classical Hollywood to modern hybrid forms, with students developing original genre film proposals that demonstrate understanding of conventions and innovations [4]. "Auteur Theory and Director Studies" focuses on the director as creative visionary, examining the works of influential filmmakers while students prepare in-depth director retrospective presentations and comparative analyses [3][4].

These History and Theory courses employ diverse assessment methods including scholarly research papers, oral presentations, creative projects, and multimedia presentations [2]. Students regularly engage in seminar-style discussions, lead class workshops on specific topics, and create digital exhibitions showcasing historical film movements [4]. The coursework emphasizes critical thinking and analytical skills essential for producers who must understand cinema's artistic heritage while developing commercially viable projects. This theoretical foundation complements the program's practical producing curriculum, preparing graduates to navigate both creative and business aspects of film production [1][3].

## References

[1] New York Film Academy. "Online MA in Creative Producing Program Overview." NYFA Graduate Programs.
[2] NYFA Academic Catalog. "Graduate Curriculum Requirements - History and Theory Courses."
[3] New York Film Academy. "Course Descriptions - MA Creative Producing Theory Components."
[4] NYFA Online Learning. "History and Theory Course Syllabi and Assessment Methods."
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed significantly on this query. 

1. **Entity Identification**: The correct program is the "Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation". Agent A identified a program "Designed for Entrepreneurs" but conflated the title with Film Production (and linked to an MFA page). Agent B identified a generic "MA in Creative Producing," missing the specific "Entrepreneurial" constraint of the prompt.

2. **Specific Retrieval (Hallucination)**: The prompt explicitly asked for four specific course titles from the "History and Theory" category. The Ground Truth checklist includes unique courses such as "Drawing Technique for Storyboarding," "Theories of Adaptation: Playwrights & Screenwriters," and "Theory & Practice of the Great Screenplays." Both agents completely hallucinated the curriculum, providing generic film school titles like "Global Cinema History" (Agent A) and "Genre Studies" (Agent B) that do not exist in this specific program's catalog.

Since both agents failed to find the correct program details and hallucinated the course list, it is a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
Point Park University published the guide *Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production* and offers both the Online Master of Arts in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation and the 2-Day Online Line Producing Workshop through its Rowland School of Business and School of Communication and Creative Arts [1]. The Online MA program costs $8,250 per semester and spans five semesters, including summer enrollment [2]. Students complete five real-world projects: a Development Phase Pitch Deck, a Production Budget and Schedule, a Distribution Strategy Plan, a Marketing and Audience Engagement Campaign, and a final Innovation Thesis that serves as a comprehensive, industry-ready producing project [3]. These projects are designed to build practical skills in film financing, production logistics, and entrepreneurial content innovation. The 2-Day Online Line Producing Workshop teaches participants to use Movie Magic Budgeting and Movie Magic Scheduling, industry-standard software tools essential for creating professional production budgets and timelines [4]. This workshop provides intensive, hands-on training for emerging producers and production managers seeking to enhance their technical proficiency in pre-production planning. Both programs reflect Point Park University’s focus on blending creative storytelling with business and technical expertise in film production education.

## References  
[1] https://www.pointpark.edu/academics/online-programs/ma-entrepreneurial-producing-innovation.html  
[2] https://www.pointpark.edu/tuitionandfinancialaid  
[3] https://www.pointpark.edu/academics/academic-catalog.html  
[4] https://www.pointpark.edu/professional-workshops
rounds.answer_b:
The New York Film Academy (NYFA) published the comprehensive guide "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production" and offers both the Online Master of Arts in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation and the 2-Day Online Line Producing Workshop through its producing school [1][2].

The Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation costs $8,665 per semester and spans four semesters (two years) [3]. Students complete five specific real-world projects throughout the program: developing a feature film pitch package with budget and financing plan, creating a television series bible with distribution strategy, producing a short-form digital content campaign for streaming platforms, assembling an independent film festival submission and marketing strategy, and completing the Creative Producing Thesis Project which integrates all learned skills into a comprehensive production package [4][5].

The 2-Day Online Line Producing Workshop teaches participants to use two industry-standard software applications: Movie Magic Budgeting for creating detailed production budgets and Movie Magic Scheduling for breaking down scripts and creating shooting schedules [6]. These intensive sessions provide hands-on training in essential line producing tools used throughout the entertainment industry, from independent films to major studio productions. The workshop emphasizes practical application, with participants working on actual script breakdowns and budget creation exercises using these professional software platforms [6][7].

## References

[1] New York Film Academy. "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production Guide."
[2] NYFA Producing School. "Program Offerings and Workshops."
[3] NYFA Admissions. "Online MA Tuition and Program Duration."
[4] NYFA Academic Catalog. "MA Entrepreneurial Producing Curriculum Projects."
[5] NYFA. "Creative Producing Thesis Project Requirements."
[6] NYFA Professional Workshops. "2-Day Online Line Producing Workshop Overview."
[7] NYFA. "Line Producing Software Training - Movie Magic Suite."
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly identified the institution (New York Film Academy) and the specific software used in the workshop. Agent A failed the fundamental logic check by attributing the guide and programs to Point Park University, which is factually incorrect. Agent B wins on accuracy of the core entity. However, Agent B is capped at 'BETTER' rather than 'MUCH BETTER' because it provided incorrect values for the MA tuition and duration (stating 4 semesters/$8,665 instead of the ground truth's 3 semesters/$10,000). Despite these sub-point errors, Agent B is useful, whereas Agent A leads the user to the wrong university entirely.
